rulings.Case Title: JOHN BROSNAN VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Bruce M. Lubarsky's, for the Law Office of Bruce M. Lubarsky, motion to be relieved as counsel for defendant Civic Center Auto Parts is granted. The motion was properly served on defendant, whose registered agent consents to such withdrawal and no opposition was filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: OMAR BARSTAD et al VS. LAMONS GASKET COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 9, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant The Wm. Powell Company's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's negligence and products liability causes of action are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Earl Daniel's declaration and the deposition testimony of William McClure and James Hengelhold create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (packing) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Earl Daniel's declaration and the deposition testimony of William McClure and James Hengelhold are overruled. Defendant's objections to the deposition testimony of George Springs are sustained. The Court did not need to consider the declaration of Charles Ay, but if it had, defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration would be sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
